Academy.seriesEmpty: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Learning paths are coming soon. In the meantime, browse articles by topic:

categoryIntros.multisig: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Multisig wallets require multiple signatures to authorize a transaction. SSP enforces 2-of-2 on every signed action: the browser extension and the mobile SSP Key app must both approve. This page collects the deep-dives that explain how the model works, what attacks it stops, and where its limits sit.
categoryIntros.getting-started: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Brand-new to crypto, or returning after a long break? Start here. These guides cover wallets, addresses, transactions, gas, mempools, and finality — the vocabulary you need before you touch real funds.
categoryIntros.security: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Self-custody is hard because attacks are creative. This collection walks through the threats that actually catch users: phishing pages, fake support DMs, malicious dApps, lost seeds, and how SSP's 2-of-2 model neutralises most of them.
categoryIntros.how-to: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Practical, screenshot-driven walkthroughs for the things SSP users do every day: sending transactions across chains, connecting to dApps via WalletConnect, swapping tokens, and recovering from a lost device.
categoryIntros.coin-guides: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Each coin or chain SSP supports has its own quirks — UTXO vs. account-based, native multisig vs. ERC-4337, fee dynamics, finality times. This collection covers them one at a time.
categoryIntros.defi: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP supports DeFi via ERC-4337 Account Abstraction on EVM chains. These articles explain what AA is, what dApps you can use safely from a multisig wallet, and how SSP keeps you in control while letting you participate in DeFi protocols.
categoryIntros.news-explained: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Beyond the headlines: the regulatory shifts, exchange failures, protocol incidents, and standards updates that actually affect self-custody users — explained without hype.

Guide.stepsSubtitle: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Follow these detailed instructions to set up your secure crypto wallet

0.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Visit Extension Store
0.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Open your browser and navigate to the SSP Wallet Extension page for your browser (Chrome, Firefox, Brave)

1.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Install Extension
1.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Click 'Add to Chrome/Firefox' or 'Install' button. A pop-up window will appear asking if you want to add SSP Wallet. Click 'Add' or 'Install' to complete installation.

2.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Open Wallet
2.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Locate the SSP wallet logo in your browser and click on it. Follow the prompts to get started.

3.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Create Password
3.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Create your password. Please carefully read the SSP Wallet Disclaimer and acknowledge your agreement.

4.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Create Wallet
4.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Click 'Create Wallet' to initialize your new wallet.

5.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Backup Seed Phrase
5.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Click 'Show Mnemonic Wallet Seed Phrase.' Store the seed phrase securely and confirm that you have backed it up.

6.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Finalize Setup
6.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Click 'Create Wallet' to finalize the process. Your browser wallet is now ready!

0.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Download Mobile App
0.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Download SSP Key on your mobile device and open the application.

1.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Start Synchronization
1.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Click on 'Synchronize Key' to begin the setup process.

2.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Set Key Password
2.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Set an SSP Key password and confirm it. Then, click 'Setup Key.'

3.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Backup Key Seed
3.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Click 'Show Mnemonic Key Seed Phrase.' Store the key seed phrase securely and confirm backup.

4.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Complete Key Setup
4.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Click 'Setup Key.' SSP Key now serves as second authentication factor for your SSP Wallet.

5.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Scan QR Code
5.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Click 'Scan Code' to synchronize your SSP Key with SSP Wallet. Scan the QR code displayed on your browser.

6.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Approve Synchronization
6.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Click 'Approve Synchronization' and confirm with your Key password. You'll be notified of successful sync.

0.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Secure Your Seed Phrases
0.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Store both wallet and key seed phrases in a secure, offline location. Never share them with anyone.

1.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Use Strong Passwords

2.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Keep Mobile Secure
2.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Ensure your mobile device has screen lock and consider using biometric authentication.

Support.faqSubtitle: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Find quick answers to the most common questions about SSP Wallet

0.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What is SSP Wallet?
0.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Wallet is a true 2-of-2 multisignature system consisting of two components: SSP Wallet (browser extension) and SSP Key (mobile 2FA app). Both devices are required to authorize transactions, providing unmatched security for your crypto assets across 15+ blockchains.

1.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ How do I install SSP Wallet?
1.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ You can install SSP Wallet as a Chrome extension from the Chrome Web Store. Visit our download page and follow our comprehensive setup guide for step-by-step instructions.

2.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Is SSP Wallet free to use?
2.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Yes, SSP Wallet is completely free to download and use. We don't charge any fees for the wallet itself, though you'll still pay network transaction fees when making blockchain transactions.

3.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What cryptocurrencies does SSP Wallet support?
3.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Wallet supports 15+ blockchains including Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Litecoin (LTC), Zcash (ZEC), Ravencoin (RVN), Dogecoin (DOGE), Bitcoin Cash (BCH), Flux (FLUX), Polygon (MATIC), BSC, Avalanche (AVAX), and Base. For EVM-compatible chains, we support all ERC-20 tokens and you can import your own custom token lists. Solana support is coming soon.

4.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Where can I find the complete list of supported assets?
4.answerBefore: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ For a comprehensive and up-to-date list of all natively supported chains and tokens, please refer to our official
4.answerAfter: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ . This spreadsheet is regularly updated with new additions and contains detailed information about each supported asset.

5.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Can I use SSP Wallet with dApps and DeFi?
5.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Yes! SSP Wallet features full WalletConnect v2 (Reown) integration, allowing you to connect to thousands of decentralized applications across all supported EVM chains. You can use DeFi protocols, NFT marketplaces, and other dApps while maintaining the security of the 2-of-2 multisignature system.

6.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What are FluxNodes and how can I use them?
6.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ FluxNodes are nodes in the Flux network that help secure and maintain the blockchain. With SSP Wallet, you can easily set up and manage Fluxnodes, monitor their performance, and claim rewards directly through the wallet interface. Fluxnodes require a collateral amount of FLUX tokens and provide passive income through network rewards.

7.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Does SSP Wallet support fiat onboarding and offboarding?
7.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Yes! SSP Wallet supports fiat onboarding and offboarding through integrated partners. You can buy cryptocurrencies directly with your credit card, bank transfer, or other payment methods, and sell your crypto back to fiat currency. This feature is available in supported regions and requires KYC verification.

8.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Can I swap cryptocurrencies within SSP Wallet?
8.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Yes, SSP Wallet includes built-in crypto swap functionality. You can easily exchange one cryptocurrency for another across supported blockchains through our integrated swap partners. The swap feature provides competitive rates and executes swaps securely while maintaining your private keys.

9.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Can I export my transaction history?
9.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Yes! SSP Wallet supports CSV export of your transaction history. You can export detailed transaction records for accounting, tax purposes, or personal record-keeping. The CSV export includes transaction dates, amounts, addresses, fees, and other relevant details for each supported blockchain.

10.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ How can I request a new coin to be added?
10.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ We encourage users to reach out to our support team or contribute directly on our GitHub repository to request new cryptocurrency support. We evaluate each request based on community demand, technical feasibility, and security considerations.

11.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Is there a mobile wallet available?
11.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Good news! We are actively working on expanding SSP Wallet to mobile platforms. Currently, SSP Wallet consists of a browser extension (SSP Wallet) and a mobile 2FA app (SSP Key). A full mobile wallet experience is in development.

12.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Can I use SSP Wallet on multiple devices?
12.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Yes, you can use SSP Wallet on multiple devices. To set up on a new device, you'll need to restore your wallet and key using the corresponding seed phrases. Each device requires proper setup and synchronization.

13.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Is there a transaction limit with SSP Wallet?
13.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Wallet doesn't impose transaction limits. However, you're subject to the blockchain network limits and your available balance. Some networks may have minimum transaction amounts or maximum transaction sizes based on their protocols.

14.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Can I use SSP Wallet offline?
14.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Wallet requires an internet connection for most operations including transaction broadcasting, balance updates, and synchronization between devices. While you can view some cached information offline, you cannot send transactions without connectivity.

0.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ How secure is SSP Wallet?
0.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Wallet uses multi-signature technology with mobile-integrated authentication, providing two-key protection. We use cutting-edge encryption and don't store any of your data - everything is stored locally and controlled by you.

1.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What is SSP Key and why do I need it?
1.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Key is a mobile app that holds the second private key required for the 2-of-2 multisignature system. Every transaction must be signed by both your browser extension AND your mobile device. This ensures your funds remain secure even if one device is compromised. SSP Key is available for iOS 15.1+ and Android 7+.

2.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Can I recover my wallet if I lose my device?
2.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Yes, you can recover your wallet using your seed phrase. Both your wallet and key have separate seed phrases that should be stored securely offline. Never share your seed phrases with anyone.

3.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Does SSP Wallet collect my personal data?
3.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ No, SSP Wallet is committed to not storing any data information. Your private keys, transaction history, and personal data remain entirely under your control and are stored locally on your devices.

4.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What are deterministic builds and why do they matter?
4.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Deterministic builds ensure that identical source code produces identical binary outputs. SSP Wallet uses Docker-based deterministic builds that enable anyone to independently verify that the distributed extension matches exactly what was built from the published source code. This eliminates potential supply chain attacks and provides cryptographic proof of build integrity.

5.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ How can I verify the deterministic build of SSP Wallet?
5.answerIntro: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ To verify SSP Wallet builds: 1) Download the release files (Chrome/Firefox zips, SHA256SUMS, SHA256SUMS.asc) from

6.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Who signs the SSP Wallet releases?
6.answerBefore: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ All SSP Wallet releases are cryptographically signed by security@runonflux.io using GPG signatures. Our public key is available on
6.answerAfter: __TODO_TRANSLATE__  for verification. The deterministic build process includes Docker-based isolated environments and comprehensive checksums (SHA256SUMS) that are also GPG-signed to ensure authenticity and integrity.

7.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Can I use multiple SSP Keys with one SSP Wallet?
7.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ No, each SSP Wallet can only be paired with one SSP Key. The 2-of-2 multisignature system requires device-specific synchronization for public nonces and chain data. Using multiple SSP Keys would cause synchronization conflicts.

0.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Which browsers are supported?
0.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Wallet supports Google Chrome, Brave, Firefox, and other Chromium-based browsers. You can download the Chrome Web Store version for Chromium browsers or the Firefox version directly from our GitHub releases.

1.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ I'm having trouble syncing my mobile key
1.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Make sure both devices are connected to the internet, the QR code is clearly visible, and you're using the latest versions of both the browser extension and mobile app. Try restarting both applications if sync fails.

2.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ My transaction is stuck or pending
2.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Blockchain transactions can sometimes take time depending on network congestion. Check the transaction status on a blockchain explorer. If using Ethereum, you may need to increase gas fees for faster confirmation.

3.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ How do I update SSP Wallet?
3.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Browser extensions typically update automatically. You can manually check for updates in your browser's extension management page. For the mobile app, check your device's app store for updates.

4.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What is SSP Relay and how does it work?
4.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Relay is an open-source relay service that acts as a bridge between your SSP Wallet and SSP Key. It provides market information, fee data, enables transaction synchronization, and delivers push notifications. The relay enhances the user experience while maintaining security.

5.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Does SSP Wallet support Replace-By-Fee (RBF)?
5.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Yes, SSP Wallet fully supports Replace-By-Fee (RBF) functionality. You can modify transaction fees and even change recipients after broadcasting a transaction, giving you flexibility in managing your transactions on the blockchain.

6.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What fees does SSP Wallet charge?
6.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Wallet has zero fees - we don't charge anything for using the wallet. You only pay the standard blockchain network fees that go directly to miners/validators for processing your transactions. The wallet includes automatic fee selection to optimize your transaction costs.

7.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Why do some transactions fail to construct?
7.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Some transactions may fail due to blockchain size restrictions and UTXO complexity. When you have many small inputs, the transaction size can exceed network limits. In such cases, you may need to consolidate your UTXOs or break the transaction into smaller parts.

8.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Do I need to restore my wallet when changing devices?
8.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Yes, due to device-specific encryption, you must restore both your wallet and key when changing devices or making significant system changes. Keep your seed phrases secure and accessible for this process.

9.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What happens if I lose my SSP Key device?
9.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ If you lose your SSP Key device, you can restore it on a new device using your SSP Key seed phrase. Install the SSP Key app on your new device and use the seed phrase to recover access. You'll need to re-sync with your SSP Wallet browser extension after restoration.

10.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Can I backup my wallet to cloud storage?
10.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Wallet doesn't support automatic cloud backups for security reasons. Your seed phrases should be stored offline in a secure location. Never store seed phrases in cloud storage, email, or any digital format that could be compromised.

11.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What's the difference between SSP Wallet seed phrase and SSP Key seed phrase?
11.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Wallet and SSP Key each have separate seed phrases. The wallet seed phrase controls the browser extension component, while the key seed phrase controls the mobile 2FA component. Both are required for full wallet recovery and should be stored separately and securely.

12.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ How do I migrate to a new computer?
12.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ To move SSP Wallet to a new computer: 1) Install the SSP Wallet extension on the new device, 2) Use your wallet seed phrase to restore the browser extension, 3) Re-sync with your existing SSP Key mobile app. Your transaction history and settings will need to be reconfigured.

13.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ I'm getting "Synchronisation with SSP wallet needed" error on mobile
13.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ This error typically occurs when changing phones or when a blockchain is not yet synchronized with your SSP Key. To resolve: 1) Open SSP Wallet, 2) Switch to the blockchain you want to re-synchronize, 3) Click the burger menu (top right corner), 4) Select "SSP Wallet Details", 5) Confirm access with your password, 6) In the first row, find "Chain Sync with SSP Key" and click the eye icon to show the QR code, 7) Scan the QR code with your SSP Key and approve the chain synchronization.

14.question: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ I'm getting "SSP Key Public nonces do not match or SSP Key Public nonces are missing" error
14.answer: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ This error occurs when public nonces used to construct transactions are not synchronized between your SSP Wallet and SSP Key. To resolve: 1) Open the burger menu (top right corner), 2) Navigate to Settings, 3) Find the "Public Nonces" section and click "Sync Public Nonces", 4) Scan the QR code shown with your SSP Key, 5) Approve the request on your mobile device. Your public nonces are now synchronized and you can begin transacting.

fluxFoundation.metaTitle: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Case Study: Flux Foundation — Self-Custody Treasury with SSP Enterprise
fluxFoundation.metaDescription: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ How the Flux Foundation moved from scattered multisig and custom scripts to SSP Enterprise — securing the Fusion bridge and Foundation treasury with M-of-N vaults across every chain they use.
fluxFoundation.subtitle: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ How the Flux Foundation moved from scattered multisig and custom scripts to a single self-custody platform — securing the Fusion bridge and Foundation treasury without giving up keys to anyone.
fluxFoundation.heroQuote: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ We needed full control of our funds, transparency for the community, and no black box MPC sitting between us and our own treasury. SSP Enterprise was the first platform we found that matches all three — across every chain we actually use.

before.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Before: scattered multisig and custom scripts
before.intro: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ The Flux Foundation had been managing multi-party signing for years. The problem wasn't a lack of multisig — it was that nothing tied it together across chains, and the commercial alternatives all required a tradeoff the team wasn't willing to make.

0.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Scattered multisig setups
0.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Different multisig wallets across different chains, each with its own quirks, signers, and recovery procedures.

1.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Custom scripts to glue it together
1.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Internal tooling for transaction construction, signing coordination, and broadcast — maintained by hand.

2.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ No unified audit trail
2.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Approvals lived in chat threads and shared docs. Reconstructing who signed what required digging.

3.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ No middle ground in the market
3.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Custodians and MPC vendors meant trusting a black box. Off-the-shelf multisig tools were Ethereum-only.

decision.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ The decision
decision.intro: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ The team evaluated the alternatives and grouped them into three buckets. Every bucket had a dealbreaker.
decision.bucket1Description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Fireblocks, BitGo, and similar. Vendor holds part of every key.
decision.bucket2Description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Safe and the like. Ethereum-only, no native UTXO support.
decision.bucket2Verdict: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ We hold Bitcoin and Flux too.
decision.bucket3Description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Wallets without policy engine, analytics, or proper audit trail.
decision.bucket3Verdict: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ We'd be back to building tooling ourselves.
decision.conclusion: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Enterprise was the only platform that addressed all three at once: native multi-chain (UTXO and EVM), true self-custody with no vendor key share, and a built-in policy engine, analytics, and audit trail.

whatChanged.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ What changed
whatChanged.intro: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ One platform, every chain the Foundation interacts with, one consistent signing model. The custom scripts are retired. The chat-thread audit trail is replaced by attributable on-platform records. Treasury and bridge multisig live side by side under the same governance.

0.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ M-of-N vaults across every chain we touch
0.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Bitcoin, Ethereum, Flux, and the EVM L2s — same platform, same mental model, same security guarantees.

1.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Two devices per signer, no exceptions
1.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Browser extension and mobile app required for every signature. A single compromised device cannot sign.

2.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Audit trail that holds up
2.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Every proposal, approval, and rejection logged with the signer and timestamp. Replaces the chat-thread reconstruction.

3.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Self-custody, end to end
3.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ No vendor key share. No MPC infrastructure to trust. If SSP went away tomorrow, our funds remain recoverable from signer seeds.

fusionBridge.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ The Fusion bridge
fusionBridge.intro: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ The Fusion bridge is one of the most security-sensitive surfaces the Foundation operates — it coordinates value transfer between Flux and other chains, and a single signing failure could be catastrophic. It's also the workload where SSP Enterprise's model pays off most clearly.

0.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ On-chain multisig
0.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Native multisig on every chain the bridge touches — no smart-contract custodian, no relayer that can stall or front-run.

1.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Two-device signing per signer
1.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Compromising one signer's laptop or phone is not enough to push a bridge transaction. Both devices, every time.

2.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Per-signer policy controls
2.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Limits and whitelists configured at the org and vault level, enforced before any signature is collected.

3.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Auditable end to end
3.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Every approval and rejection attributed and timestamped. Community accountability without giving up self-custody.

fusionBridge.quote: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ The Fusion bridge runs on infrastructure we control end to end now. Same security guarantees we have for our own treasury, applied to one of the highest-stakes workflows we operate.

cta.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Same model. Available for your team.
cta.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ The platform the Flux Foundation runs the Fusion bridge on is the same platform available to any team that wants self-custody multisig without compromise. Free tier to start. Custom plans on request.

Enterprise.metaTitle: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ SSP Enterprise | Self-Custody Multisig for Business
Enterprise.metaDescription: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Self-custody M-of-N multisig vaults for business treasuries. Policy engine, spending limits, two-device signing, role-based access. No custodians, no MPC.
Enterprise.heroDescription: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ M-of-N multisig vaults for your organization. Every signer uses two devices — browser extension and mobile app. No custodians. No MPC. No single point of failure.

fluxBanner.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Securing the Fusion bridge and Foundation treasury across BTC, ETH, FLUX, and EVM L2s.

features.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Enterprise Features
features.subtitle: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Everything your organization needs for secure crypto management

0.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ M-of-N Multisig Vaults
0.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Configure any signing threshold for your team. 2-of-3, 3-of-5, or any combination that fits your governance model.

1.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Role-Based Access
1.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Owner, Admin, Member, and Viewer roles. Control who can propose, sign, or simply observe transactions.

2.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Two-Device Signing
2.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Every signer uses both a browser extension and a mobile app. Two keys per person, M-of-N across your team.

3.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Multi-Chain Support
3.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin, Dogecoin, Flux, Polygon, BSC, Base, Avalanche, and more. One platform for all chains.

4.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Transaction Proposals
4.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Propose transactions with full details. Team members review, approve, or reject. Broadcast only when threshold is met.

5.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Complete Audit Trail
5.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Every action logged permanently. Member changes, vault operations, transaction approvals — full accountability.

policy.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Transaction Policy Engine
policy.subtitle: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Granular controls that cascade from organization to vault to individual signer. Stricter policy always wins.
policy.tierFooter: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Policies merge top-down — the stricter limit at any tier is enforced

0.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Spending Limits
0.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Per-transaction, daily, weekly, and monthly limits. Set in USD or native token. Aggregate and per-token controls.

1.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Address Whitelist
1.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Three modes: disabled, warn, or enforce. Label addresses, restrict by chain. Block unknown destinations entirely.

2.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Time-Lock Delays
2.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Configurable waiting period before broadcast. Trigger automatically above a USD threshold. Cancel before execution.

3.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Admin Approval
3.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Require admin sign-off on high-value transactions. Set a USD threshold — anything above it needs explicit approval.

4.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Per-Signer Overrides
4.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Set individual spending limits for each team member. Junior traders get tighter controls, senior execs get higher limits.

5.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Velocity Tracking
5.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Rolling 30-day spending windows per vault, per signer, and per token. Automatic enforcement with TTL-based cleanup.

6.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Emergency Vault Freeze
6.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Instantly freeze any vault with dual-device WK signing. Blocks all proposals until unfrozen. Incident response in seconds.

7.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ 3-Tier Policy Hierarchy
7.description: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Organization defaults cascade to vaults and signers. Vault-level overrides refine the rules. Stricter policy always wins.

security.title: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ 5-Layer Security
security.subtitle: __TODO_TRANSLATE__ Defense in depth — every layer must be compromised for a breach
